body:
DIRECTIVE: URGENT — LIVE CALIBRATION STALL ON SLS(34). Priority over all pending mandate/polish work. Manuel's live USDJPY M5 session freezes right after 'calibration starting' and reads as stuck. This is a real regression: earlier builds calibrated flawlessly. Diagnose and fix, minimal blast radius, before resuming any doctrine work.

== WHAT WE KNOW (from your own SLS33/34 changelog + Manuel's log) ==
 - The post-init sequence regressed at SLS(33)/WARRANT#15: the ghost-gate P1-baseline rescue scan was widened from 1 fixed-threshold check to a 5-candidate sweep, but as first shipped it RE-RAN THE ENTIRE OOS BAR SCAN ONCE PER CANDIDATE (5x), and that function sits inside a while(ghostRetry<=MAX_GHOST_RETRY) loop of up to 9 iterations across 3 call sites. Effective worst case ~5x9x3 = up to 135 full OOS bar-scans where the old flawless sequence ran it once. On Manuel's 8GB machine that reads as a freeze -- it is not a hang, it is ~100x+ the work.
 - SLS(34)/WARRANT#16 was your hotfix: restructured to a SINGLE O(n) pass bucketing all 5 candidates per bar. Correct fix in the SOURCE.
 - BUT Manuel's terminal still stalls on SLS(34). The uploaded SLS(34).mq4 contains the O(n) fix, yet the running session behaves like the O(5n) SLS(33). Strong hypothesis: the .ex4 ON THE CHART is a STALE COMPILE -- source has WARRANT#16, the compiled binary on the chart does not. MT4 runs the .ex4, not the .mq4.
 - Relay note: your mailbox only delivered up to SLS(32). SLS(33)/(34) were built locally and never pushed. So there is no relay-verified compile of 33/34 -- exactly the builds now misbehaving.

== ACTIONS, IN ORDER ==
1. VERIFY THE COMPILE. Recompile the current SLS(34) source cleanly in MetaEditor and confirm the produced .ex4 timestamp is newer than the one on Manuel's chart. Deliver the freshly-compiled SLS(34).ex4 through the relay (push it to files/from-agent) so there is a verified binary, not a local-only one. Confirm 0 errors and report the exact .ex4 build time.

2. IF A CLEAN SLS(34) STILL STALLS (O(n) fix present and compiled but session still grinds on 8GB): the residual multiplier is the retry loop itself. Reduce MAX_GHOST_RETRY from 9 to a small value (try 3) as a bounded, one-line, reversible change -- this cuts the worst-case nesting ~3x directly. File it as a Change Warrant with the predicted calibration-time reduction. Also add a wall-clock budget guard to the rescue-scan call site: if cumulative ghost-retry time exceeds a threshold (e.g. 20s), break and fall back to the P1 baseline threshold, so this path can NEVER read as frozen again regardless of hardware. This mirrors the existing 150ms coordinate-descent budget pattern already in the file (the T1 time-budget idiom) -- reuse it, do not invent a new one.

3. INSTRUMENT THE SEQUENCE. Add a single concise log line at entry to the ghost rescue-scan reporting candidate-count x retry-count and elapsed ms, so the next stall is visible in the journal instead of looking like a freeze. Keep the elegant one-line house style.

4. REGRESSION GUARD. Add to the ledger a performance-regression check: any new loop/scan introduced in the calibration path must state its big-O cost and worst-case call count in its Change Warrant. WARRANT#15 shipped a 100x cost increase with no cost prediction -- that is the gap that let this reach live. Going forward, calibration-path cost is a first-class predicted-and-scored quantity, exactly like the orthogonal metric.

== HARD RULES ==
Minimal, reversible edits only; do not touch the search LOGIC (results must be identical, only cost changes). Zero compile errors before delivery. Push the verified .ex4 through the relay this time -- no local-only builds for anything Manuel runs live. Report: root cause confirmed (stale compile vs residual retry cost), the exact fix, predicted vs any measurable calibration-time delta, and the new .ex4 build timestamp. Do NOT resume mandate/doctrine/polish work until Manuel confirms the live session calibrates without stalling. Live trading still requires Manuel's explicit go-ahead; a stall fix is not a go-live.

This is the flawless post-init sequence Manuel remembers, regressed by one over-eager scan. Restore it.
